entry.title: Currency conversion insufficient privilege
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Maybe I'm doing something wrong, but I am at a total loss currently. I have a TEST space and a PROD space and a SAP space. In the SAP space I have my currency conversion objects, which I have shared with both the TEST and PROD space using the currency conversion views creator built into SAP Datasphere. I have also created the currency conversion views from the SAP space in the TEST space and put them in a package that I import in the PROD space.

In the screenshots below you can see how I shared the currency conversion tables with the other space

And this is how I created the views from the SAP space in the TEST space.

Everything works as it should in the TEST space, but when I import all of my packages into the PROD space, I get a insufficient privilege error. I can fix this by basically deleting the currency conversion columns and creating them again, but if I import the package again because of changes in the TEST space, I have to do this all over again. So this isn't a long term solution.

Does anyone maybe know what I am doing wrong or how I can make sure the currency conversion doesnt break when I transport objects between spaces.

entry.title: Re: New Process for Changing Display Names in SAP Community
entry.content:
Hi, @Marian_Zeis:

As you probably noticed, names didn't change. 

RE: Why this process changed:

1) People can now create a username when they create an SAP account, so there's no need for them to create a separate username just for SAP Community.

2) Since the username is in the SAP Universal ID account manager, it can be made available to any SAP platform (such as ours).

3) Previously, the account manager would display first and last name by default. People can now create a username that gives them more anonymity (should they wish).

Kind regards,

--Jerry

Filip

entry.title: Re: How to do create with Odata V4 with Guid on Item with...
entry.content:
I never tried to create both the header and an item at once, only the header first and then the item. You can try the following:

const binding = oExtensionApi
    .getModel()
    .bindList("/MissionNeeds/MissionNeedsItem");
const payLoad = {
    "MissionNeedsID" : selectedLine.MissionNeedsID
    };

binding.create(payLoad);

// instead of attachCreateCompleted i use created
binding.created().then(() => {
    const itemList = oExtensionApi.getModel()
        .bindList("/MissionNeeds/MissionNeedsItem('" + selectedLine.MissionNeedsID + "')/_Item");
    const itemBinding = itemList.create(
        { 
            "MissionNeedsID" : selectedLine.MissionNeedsID, 
            "Product": selectedLine.Product, 
            "Quantity": "2.000", 
            "Unit": "ST",
        });
});

 However with your current Behavior Definition, this won't work because fields of your child entity being readonly. Only MissionNeedsID will be saved, the other fields will have default value. You can fix this by using readonly : update

field ( readonly : update )
  MissionNeedsItemID,
  MissionNeedsID,
  Product,
  Quantity,
  Unit,
  CreatedAt,
  CreatedBy,
  LastChangedAt,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy;

entry.title: Re: F110 REGUH-XAVIS Separate Payment Advice indicator is...
entry.content:
This solves your issue.

1224437 - PMW: When are separate payment advice notes created?

A separate payment advice note is created by print, e-mail, or fax if:
- 'Always pyt adv' is entered in the company code for the payment method (transaction FBZP), Since the system determines only during data medium creation whether a separate payment advice note is required, payment advice note creation must take place after data medium creation. In this case, the value XAVIS in the table REGUH is set to 'X'. The payment advice note report RFFOAVIS_FPAYM processes only records with REGUH-XAVIS = 'X'. The field XAVIS can be 'X' only if Payment advice check box is active at Payment method at company code level in FBZP.

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement MT940
entry.content:
Hello @Chanaporn 

Thanks for the clear example and screenshot. You're working with MT940 bank statement integration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and your goal is to handle clearing logic between bank balance, technical, and client-specific GL accounts, particularly distinguishing Bank Incoming (1000002) and Bank Balance (1000001).

Let’s go through it

In Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement, the system assigns:

  • GL account for symbol ++++++++30 = “Other Bank Transfer”
  • GL account for symbol ++++++++90 = “Technical Account”

This means,

  • These accounts are part of SAP’s standard posting model, used to balance entries during intermediate clearing in the MT940 process.
  • However, your custom account 1000002 (Bank Incoming) is not being hit because the system doesn’t see it as part of the auto-posting logic via the account symbols defined.

Solution Approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Since you're in Public Cloud, your configuration is limited to:

  • Using Account Symbols & Posting Rules
  • Managed in: “Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement” (SSCUI ID: 100297)
  • You cannot build custom Z-logic, but you can adjust mapping if permitted.

 

Suggestions

  1. Check Account Symbol Mapping
  • Go to: Define Account Symbols
  • Add a new symbol for “Bank Incoming” (e.g. BANKIN)
  • Assign it to GL account 1000002
  1. Adjust Posting Rule
    • Go to: Assign External Transaction Types to Posting Rules
    • For incoming transaction code (e.g. NTRF, NTRI, etc.), assign a new posting rule that references BANKIN instead of +30
  1. Check Posting Specification
    • Ensure the correct combination of:
      • Bank Subaccount: 1000002 (Bank Incoming)
      • Bank Main Account: 1000001 (Bank Balance)
    • Use Manage Incoming Payment Files to simulate behaviour

Please note!

  • ++++++++30 and ++++++++90 are SAP-controlled logic for interbank clearing and should not be modified directly in the posting model.
  • However, you can build alternate account symbols + posting rules and assign them per External Transaction Code in MT940.

Summary

Goal

Solution

Use 1000002 for Bank Incoming

Create a custom account symbol & assign it in the posting rule

Avoid the system defaulting to & 30

Map your MT940 transaction to a new posting rule

Public Cloud restriction

No custom BAdIs work via SSCUI and posting rule logic only

entry.title: Re: Error "Duplicate column names in the result" with col...
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you very much for your reply.

"So I am guessing that you are coding this SQL in a universe derived table.  Please correct me if I am wrong."
--> I don't use derived tables. Anyway, the error occurs with all kinds of tables (origin tables, alias tables, derived tables) in IDT and WebI (there is no error in BigQuery with exact same queries).

"When coding a derived table, as you mentioned, you need to provide a unique alias, like TABLE_1.COL_NAME as COL_NAME1.  Help me to understand why this would not be sustainable. "
--> This solution is not sustainable because I cannot add an alias through a custom SQL in WebI, I have to add the alias in the SQL definition of the object in the business layer of IDT (we have hundreds of documents with tens of queries each that encounter this problem).
Yet, I cannot use such objects (with an alias in their SQL definition) in the filter section in WebI, which is a major problem for us.

It seems that the problem is related to the connection driver, and/or the way IDT is configured (.prm files for instance).
Indeed, the problem did not occur on our previous platform BO 4.2 SP9 Patch 7 using a generic ODBC driver (which is not supported by SAP). We currently use BO 4.3 SP4 Patch 6 with native ODBC BigQuery driver.
Can you confirm that you use a native BigQuery ODBC driver as well?

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 25.6
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am happy to announce that a new release of the Mobile Development Kit(MDK) is available for all Mobile Services customers and can be downloaded on the SAP Software Center and will soon be available on community Download page as well.

The SAP Mobile Services Client has also been updated to 25.6.0 both in the Google Play Store and in Apple App Store.

SAP Mobile Development Kit enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / VSCode extension) and run it natively on Mobile (Android & iOS).  

Below enhancements are available only on iOS and Android platforms:

  • New UI enhancements
    •  Support Grouping in Object Table Control
    • Support Body Elements in Card Collection Control
    • Donut Chart Support
    • Support Customizing Placeholder in Form Cell List Picker Control
    • Bottom Sheet Support for Popover Menu (Android Only)
    • Support Top Caption in Form Cell Segmented Control (iOS Only)
  • API and Syntax enhancements
    • Support Lock and Wipe Settings in the MDK Client
    • Support Device Compliance in MDK Client
    • Expose Generate Content API from Gemini LLM
    • Simplify Application Launcher and Splash Screen Generation in MDK Branded Client
    • Support for getCell in DataTableRowProxy

Support Grouping in Object Table Control 

MDK now supports grouping items in an Object Table section, making it easier to get an overview of data on large list screens.

//excerpt of a page 
"Grouping": {
    "GroupingProperties": [
        "CategoryName"
    ],
    "Header": {
        "Items": [{
            "Title": "Category: {CategoryName} ",
            "Styles": {
                "Title": "DataTableGroupHeaderItem",
                "BackgroundColor": "sectionBgColor"
            }
        }]
    }
},
"_Type": "Section.Type.ObjectTable",
….

Grouping in Object Table control

For more information, refer to the Grouping documentation and its API Reference.

Support Body Elements in Card Collection Control

The Card Collection control provides brief, related pieces of information and serves as an entry point, teaser, or preview for more detailed content. By tapping on a card, users can select it and navigate to a dedicated page with expanded information.

We have now introduced Body Elements in the Collection control. The card body is the central part of the card, used to display additional information alongside the content shown in the card header. It is a separate container that can hold various components, with a flexible height to accommodate a reasonable number of UI elements.

Following contents can currently displayed in Card Body Elements currently supports:

  • Text
  • Label Bar
  • Space
  • Separator
//excerpt of a page
   "Body": {
        "_Type": "Card.Type.Body",
        "OnPress": "/MDK2504/Rules/Card/ShowToastMessage.js",
        "Contents": [
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.Text",
                "Text": "The card body is the central part of the card that is used to display additional information alongside the content shown in the card header.",
                "NumberOfLines": 5
            },
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.LabelBar",
                "Layout": {
                    "LayoutType": "Vertical"
                },
                "ItemSeparator": true,
                "Items": "/MDK2504/Rules/Card/CardStaticContentLabelBarItems.js"
            },
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.Space",
                "NumberOfSpacings": 3
            },
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.Separator",
                "_Style": "card-label-2"
            }
        ]
    },

Body Elements in Card Collection Control

For more information, refer to th Card Collection documentation and its API Reference.

Donut Chart Support  

You can now use the Donut Chart type to visualize data in a new way that emphasizes various aspects of the dataset. Donut charts are ideal for displaying proportions of categorical data in a part-to-whole analysis. They take a single dataset and represent the ratios in a circular format.

Support for the Donut Chart type is available in the Analytic Card Collection, Chart Content Section, and Object Header Chart view UI controls.

Donut Chart in iOS

Donut Chart in Android

For more information, see these documentation on Donut chart support in Analytic Card Collection, Chart Content Section, and Object Header.

Support Customizing Placeholder in Form Cell List Picker Control

We have introduced a new property, Placeholder, for the List Picker Form Cell control to customize the placeholder text when no value is selected. This property provides contextual cues to guide business users in making their selections.

 
//excerpt of a page 
{
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.ListPicker",
    "_Name": "FormCellListPicker0",
    "PickerPrompt": "Please select one single item",
    "PlaceHolder": "Select Item",
    "IsEditable": true,
    "PickerItems": [
        "Alpha",
        "Bravo",
        --
    ]
}​
 
 

For more information, see this  documentation.

Bottom Sheet Support for Popover Menu (Android Only)

A new property, IsBottomSheet, has been introduced for the Popver Menu Action.

  • When set to true, the popover is displayed as a bottom sheet.
  • When set to false or not defined, the popover is displayed as a dialog.
{
    "PopoverItems": [{
            "Icon": "sap-icon://synchronize",
            "OnPress": "/MDK2504/Actions/Service/SyncStartedMessage.action",
            "Title": "Sync Changes"
        }
    ],
    "_Type": "Action.Type.PopoverMenu",
    "IsBottomSheet": true
}

 

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support Top Caption in Form Cell Segmented Control (iOS Only)

Prior to this release, MDK displayed segmented Form Cell controls on iOS as single-line buttons, which  would dynamically switch to a stacked layout. With this release, we are introducing a new property for the Segmented Control: CaptionPosition. When this property is set to Top, the control will always use stacked layout.

{
    "CaptionPosition": "Top", //"Adaptive"
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SegmentedControl",
    "_Name": "FormCellSegmentedControl2",
    "Caption": "Top Caption",
    "ApportionsSegmentWidthsByContent": false,
    "Segments": [
        "Low",
        "Medium",
        "High"
    ]
}

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support Lock and Wipe Settings in the MDK Client

We are adding support for Locking and Wiping settings on MDK client. This new feature will significantly improve security and protect business users' sensitive information, helping safeguard them against unauthorized access or misuse of their devices.

  • Lock (users): Lock users on the specified device and force them to re-authenticate to continue using the app.
  • Wipe (device registration) : Lock the user and clear all app data (including any local changes) on the device. User has to re-onboard to continue using the app.

 Administrator can lock users and wipe device registrations through the following options:

  • Initiate Locking or Wiping actions directly from the mobile services Admin UI using toggle buttons.

 

  • Set up offline locking/wiping policies that automatically lock or wipe the device when the configured number of days expires. Number of days are based on how long the app is offline (without connecting to server).

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support Device Compliance in MDK Client

MDK introduces Device Compliance Detection, a security feature that allows app developers to perform various security checks to determine whether a device has been compromised.

Administrators must enable the Device Compliance Detection policies in the mobile services Admin UI to ensure compliance reports are sent to Mobile Services.  

When the Compliance Policy is enabled in the Mobile Services Admin UI:

  • Detection is automatically performed during app login and each time the app is restarted.
  • The compliance status for each registration will be visible in the Mobile Services Admin UI.

For more information, see this  documentation.  

To check the status of your device, we have added a new Client API isDeviceCompliant(). 

  • If it returns true, the device is compliant. 
  • If it returns false, the device is non-compliant or has been compromised.
var isRootDevices = clientAPI.isDeviceCompliant();

Support Generate Content API from Gemini LLM

In MDK’s previous release 24.11, we introduced support for leveraging the Chat Completion LLM API.

In this release, we are now introducing a new MDK Action called Generate Content, which connects to SAP AI Core and explicitly supports integration with Gemini Generate Content API, enabling seamless content generation. This API allows developers to create dynamic, structured content for articles, reports, and multimedia outputs, leveraging advanced AI models like Gemini 1.0 Pro and Gemini 1.5 Pro.

Instead of calling an LLM API provided directly by an LLM service provider, SAP AI Core offers a unified way to access LLM APIs from the most popular LLM service platforms. SAP AI Core manages the AI model and ensures scalable deployment in enterprise applications, maintaining compliance and security.

 The Generate Content LLM API can address various scenarios, including:

  • Text Input: Submit text prompts to the API to receive AI-generated responses.
  • Input: Enhance the interaction by including images, allowing for a multimodal conversational experience.
  • Function Calling: Enable the AI model to invoke specific functions or execute commands based on the conversation’s context.
{
  "_Type": "Action.Type.AICore.Generate.Content",
  "Target": {
    "Service": "/MDKDemoApp/Services/OpenAI.service",
    "Path": "/models/gemini-1.5-pro:generateContent"
  },
  "Properties": {
    "Contents": []
  },
  "ShowActivityIndicator": true,
  "ActivityIndicatorText": "Processing ...",
  "ActionResult": {
    "_Name": "GenerateContent"
  },
  "OnFailure": "/MDKDemoApp/Actions/Failure.action"
}​

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Simplify Application Launcher and Splash Screen Generation in MDK Branded Client

Prior to this release, the effort required to customize Application Launcher icon and Splash screen is non-trivial. A developer has to generate these icons and screens using different ways and different sizes for different platforms as documented here.

We have simplified and unified generating these application launcher icon and Splash screen for developers to provide these images under a single location.

A new property, AppImages, has been introduced in MDKProject.json , where you can mention the file details in the given properties.

"AppImages": {
  "Launcher": {
    "Logo": "launcher",
    "Background": "android_launcher_background", // Android only
  },
  "Splash": {
    "Logo": "splash_logo",
    "Background": "background", // iOS only
    "LogoBackground": "android_splash_background", // Android only
  }
}

All those files should be placed under yourMDKApp.mdkproject>App_Resources>MDK>Images

Please note you can still continue previous way of customization as mentioned in our documentation. However, in order to use this new way, we highly suggest to remove any customization or override files added for the launcher and splash screens in the App_Resources and App_Resources_Merge folders for both iOS and Android.

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support for getCell in DataTableRowProxy

With this release, we are introducing getCell feature in DataTableRowProxy, enabling you to retrieve a DataTableCellProxy object using the column index of the target cell. This new functionality will seamlessly let you access and interact with specific cells within a table row.

let cellParent = context.getParent();
let columnIndex = context.getColumnIndex();
let getCell = cellParent.getCell(columnIndex).getValue();

New to MDK development?
Follow  these tutorials  to get started and learn more about Mobile development kit!
I am looking forward to your feedback/comments.

entry.title: Criterio al articulo 29 de la Ley del INFONAVIT
entry.content:
Buen día,

Se implementaron las notas del articulo 29 de la Ley del INFONAVIT
1.-Se modifico la  tabla-V_T5F99K2

32 CKINF DESCUENTO DIAS PROPORCIONAL - INFONAVIT  0 No diminuir ausentismos \ incapacidades

2.-Se modifico la  tabla-V_T51P6
/381 Apor. crédito INFONAVIT 05/12/2025 12/31/9999 B 3 4

3.-Se copio la regla MXIN a ZXIN y se agregaron las reglas que indica la nota


P0370 Cálculo deducciones crédito INFO/FONA
PIT ZFM0 Grabar Z381 antes de PRDNT
PRDNT
PIT ZFM1 Cambiar Z381 despues de PRDNT
PIT ZFM2 Cambia /381 y /385

 

Se realizaron , las pruebas y funciona cuando el mes de la  fecha inicio y mes de la fecha din son iguales.

Si el mes de la fecha inicio es diferente al mes de la fecha fin marca el siguiente error.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CC-nómina no se ha reducido correctamente
Una CC-nómina no ha sido reducida completamente por importe
en tabla DDNTK
CC-nómina /381
Verifique si CC-nómina en evento de función PRDNT
todavía está en tabla IT (Clase de tratamiento 20)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

¿A alguien mas le marco este error y como lo solucionaron?

 

Gracias.

Saludos.

entry.title: ePPDS GUI - Detailed scheduling board vs Fiori - app Advanced Scheduling board
entry.content:
Intro:
In SAP PP/DS (Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling), scheduling boards are essential tools for visualizing and managing production orders, operations, and resource allocation. SAP provides two main user interfaces for this purpose:

Detailed Scheduling (DS) Board – SAP GUI: Tcode - /N/SAPAPO/CDPS0
This is the traditional tool used in the classic SAP GUI environment. It offers deep functionality for planners, including detailed Gantt chart views, drag-and-drop rescheduling, and manual intervention capabilities. It's highly configurable but has a steeper learning curve and an older UI design.

Advanced Scheduling Board – Fiori (SAP S/4HANA):
Introduced as part of the modern Fiori-based user experience in SAP S/4HANA, the Advanced Scheduling Board provides a more intuitive and visually appealing interface. It aims to simplify scheduling tasks with responsive design, easier navigation, and better integration with other Fiori apps, while still retaining core scheduling capabilities.

Main Part:

PPDS GUI - Detailed scheduling board vs Fiori Advanced Scheduling Board
Its a interactive dashboard which will give a overview of the production orders / schedule and the resource bottle neck in shopfloor.
Overall Profile - Not available as input in fiori 2022 FS02
In GUI Input screen you can give the overall profile. Inside the overall profile the planning board profile, DS Strategy profile, Heuristics profile, and more profiles will be maintained as defaulted.
Planning board Profile - This will deside which details or bar charts to be displayed in output screen. Keep the no display checkbox empty to display the charts in output.


Output showing the unselected charts.

In Fiori app Advanced planning Board we can directly mention the charts to be displayed in output instead of a profile.

DS Strategy profile - This profile defines the methodology used to schedule sequence of the orders. 

List of available strategy profile,

Same strategy profile can be found in fiori app by using the strategy button

Heuristic Profile - Not available in Fiori

Heuristics in fiori app can be accessed via Heuristics button. But not sure it not working for me.
More Profiles like Alert profile & Propagation range

Alert Profile can be maintained under settings in fiori app

Time Profile - its used to collect list of orders between a time period. In output you can see the timeline on top

 



In Fiori App you can use the horizon to change the timeline period

On input we can give work area. Using this we can group workcenter for which we are responsible to schedule orders. In Addition we can use the resource, Product, order in input to get the data accordingly.

The Output screen is divided in to two area - Object window & Item window.

Object Window
Right clicking on the resource objects will give you lot of options.

Begin with first graphical object will move the time line to first where the first order starts. Expand multiple loading will show overlapping orders in seperate lines.

Same in Fiori App

Like wise options for products objects


In Fiori

Like wise for product stocks but its not available in fiori

Timeline
Right clicking on the timeline give you options to change the view from daily to hour or weekly basis.

In Fiori we can use this zoom slider to control the same

Item Window
Right clicking on the resource item give below options


In Fiori they have grouped the menu as below
Right clicking on product item will give below options

Product items in Fiori



Stock items does not provide any options and stock chart not available in fiori

Functionalities:
Define downtimes - Not available in Fiori
On Resource object window we can define downtimes in workcenter using the define downtime options. The downtime will be shown as dark bars in output. 

After the downtime addition below are changes in timeline (dark Area)

Custom Intervals - Not available in Fiori
After doing changes we need to click save on output screen to save the orders.
Using define intervals we can maintain custom intervals on workcenter. During the interval we can not schedule orders.

After interval addition you can see the change in color. On dragging a order inside interval give a error as shown below.

DS Strategy - Only Desired date not available in Fiori
On clicking the DS strategy we can change the desired data to specific date. So whenever you click reschedule the system will ask you the date. But it will not ask dates if you drag and drop orders.


In Fiori scheduling modes - Not available as icons

Forward scheduling - On clicking the forward arrow button the forward scheduling will be activated. While dragging and dropping the orders it will be scheduled in forward direction.
Backward scheduling schedule the orders in backward. And both FS and BS will schedule orders along with find slot, Insert operation, infinite scheduling. Find slot will schedule order in between orders if capacity available. Insert operation will schedule order in between orders eventhough the capacity is not available.
Scenario:
In below example I have choosed forward scheduling and want the order to be started from 19.06.2025 midnight.
After schedule, order has started exactly as mentioned above

In below example I have choosed backward scheduling and want the order to be completed before 21.06.25 noon
After schedule its exaclty got scheduled before noon
Find slot - Consider we need to insert order in between two orders. Find slot will insert order operation in between orders if capacity is available. If capacity not availabe it will find next slot in future date and schedule it there. 
In below scenario I am draging and droping the order 3764 in between 3763 and 3761. 

Here the available capacity is 12 hrs but one order need 24 hrs to complete. As findslot will consider capacity it scheduled ther order 64 after 61.

Insert operation - This is opposite to find slot. i.e. it will insert order inbetween the orders even the capacity is not available
Done the same above schenario with insert operation and it inserted the order inbetween others even the capacity is reserved for other order.


Insert and close gaps - I will insert operation in between orders as like insert operation and close the gaps
I am applying insert and close gap for below scenario. Moving a order in between two where 48 hr capacity available for only 24 hr requirement.

For me it as not working. Will add it later. All above DS scheduling options are available in Fiori using drag drop functionality.


Heuristics - Not yet available in Fiori
To schedule operation we need to do the execute heuristics schedule sequence from function tab

 Remove backlogs will move all orders from past date to current or future dates. 

Before removing backlogs - the highlighted redline is current date and time. 17.06.25 11 o clock. So 3 orders are scheduled in past date.

After removing backlogs - Here moving the ongoing order also to future.

Schedule sequence Manually - using this we can manually schedule the order. To do this first select the workcenter and then click schedule sequence manually from heuristics.

On clicking it a new window will open with two tabs - Sequence to be planned and clipboad. At first we need to move all to clipboard. Its like work area or draft by clicking swap pages.

Later we need to drag drop orders one by one to sequence to be planned tab as per our need.

After schedule this is the output and later save it and adopt it.

Deallocate - This will remove the capacity reservations.

Resource item window options

In Fiori resource item options - Some options not available (switch source of supply)
Select objects for the same order will select the material from other charts that belongs to the header order from resource chart.

 


In Fiori

Show pegging relationship will show the relationship between the order with green arrow. The previous orders need to be completed before the successive orders. Hide pedding relationships will hide the green arrow.

In Fiori

 


Rescheduling option will schedule the orders in a sequence next to next.

In Fiori

Deallocation will remove capacity reservation and add yellow bar to order. If you drag and drop it, then again it will be rescheduled.

We can schedule another order on same day by drag drop.

In Fiori - A small black line added on bottom of deallocated order

Fixing will remove the edit access to an order and its highlighted with red colour. We can edit or schedule the order again only after unfixing it.

In Fiori - A small red line on top of fixed orders

Using create order we can create orders. Also we can delete orders

In Fiori

On execute an order will be created with APO number range and moved to Hana system to get the real number ranges and then it will be updated here. We can create orders with template, in which the data cannot be changed.
We can also change the order quantity. If we have multiple production version we can switch the source of supply.


We can set convertion indicator and remove convertion indicator. We can remove convertion indicator only before saving the changes. We can create resource, access /n/SAPAPO/MAT01
We can access product view i.e. /n/SAPAPO/RRP3

Resource overload Alert - I just done overlading the workcenter using infininte scheduling and clicked bell button and slected a error to show the overload in resource

 


Network Alert - Have moved the semifinished order behind the finished order in dates to create the Network alert. Usually the semifinshed material to be produced before the finished materials. If the sequence not followed then the SFG will not be available to produce the FG. since the network alert will be issued.

In Fiori - Alerts are shown as notification

Also we can activate the alert display from menu extras.

So that the problem items will be displayed in different colors

Conclusion:
I hope this may help someone. Please mention if there are mistakes

entry.title: Documentation Update: Service Plans and Initial Setup for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime
entry.content:
Introduction

Managing entitlements on SAP BTP can be somewhat challenging, especially if you're new to the process. For SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime, it may be even more complicated because the necessary steps vary depending on the service plan you want to use and on the commercial model of your global account.

The latest platform update introduced some improvements which give you more flexibility in managing entitlements for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime (for details, see Entitlement Management Improvements for Standard Plan). We decided to use this chance to revisit our documentation related to the initial setup of SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and to its service plans. The updated documentation not only incorporates the latest changes in the entitlement management for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime, but also brings clarity to how the setup process works overall. It provides various levels of detail, ranging from a general overview to step-by-step instructions for both the SAP BTP cockpit and the SAP BTP command line interface (btp CLI).

This post is not going to retell the content of the documentation. Instead, it's going to focus on explaining its structure and the logic behind it, making it easier to use and navigate and highlighting the most important parts.

 

Service Plans and Metering for Cloud Foundry Runtime

Some time ago, we published the Service Plans and Metering for Cloud Foundry Runtime article to help our users understand how the plans listed in the SAP Discovery Center correspond to what they find in the SAP BTP cockpit. It's a good place to start, because the article gives a general overview of the service plans for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime. We've updated it to reflect the new setup and added links to the new setup instructions. Let's take a look at some of the things that you can find here.

Interactive Diagram

In the Service section, there's a diagram detailing the plans standard and free in the consumption-based and the subscription-based commercial models. This diagram is now interactive: just hover over any rectangular node of the diagram to see its description and select it to follow the link with more information.


Interactive diagram

If you select a node in the Service Plan (SAP BTP Cockpit) or Environment Plan (SAP BTP Cockpit) columns, you'll go straight to the corresponding step-by-step setup instruction. For example, if you select the free (Environment) node, you'll go to Assigning Free Service Plan with Runtime Memory Quota for Cloud Foundry Runtime.

Not sure what is meant by "service plan" and "environment plan"? Expand the diagram captions to find a detailed explanation with screenshots.


Diagram captions (collapsed)

Next time you refer to the article, you can keep the captions collapsed so that they don't get in your way.

Tables with Service Plan Descriptions

Underneath the diagram, there are two tables in Service Plans. Each of the tables contains descriptions of the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime plans available in a particular commercial model. You'll probably only need one of these tables, so you can collapse the one that you don't need.


Service plan tables (Table 1 collapsed, Table 2 expanded)

For plans standard and free, there are now links to the corresponding setup instructions. These instructions are more high-level than the ones linked to in the diagram (more on that later).

Service Specifics

Finally, I'd like to draw your attention to the Service Specifics section, which explains how you can estimate billable consumption of runtime memory for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime.

In addition to naming the parameters that influence billable consumption, the section also includes brief explanations about how to set these parameters when deploying an app or change them if the app is already deployed. To access these explanations, expand the collapsible segments in the section.


Parameter explanations (segment about setting parameters collapsed, segment about changing parameters expanded)

 

Initial Setup Guide

We've re-written the Initial Setup article and added a lot of sub-articles that are completely new. This entire part of the documentation focuses on assigning entitlements to a subaccount and creating an instance of the Cloud Foundry runtime environment (which simultaneously creates a Cloud Foundry org) in the subaccount. Further steps related to administrating the Cloud Foundry org are out of the scope of this guide, because they are already well described in other parts of the SAP BTP documentation. You can find the links to this documentation under Next Steps.

You'll notice that there are a lot of sub-articles under Initial Setup, but the good news is that you don't have to read them all. You just need to narrow down the parts that are relevant for you based on the type of your global account, the commercial model you use, and the service plan you want. The articles are also structured to give increasingly more detailed information the further you go down the hierarchy, so you can stop at the level that's detailed enough for you.

Let's take a closer look and make it easier to decide which parts of this documentation would be relevant in your particular case, according to the criteria mentioned above.

Global Account Types and Commercial Models

This one's simple. First, choose the sub-article based on which type of global account you use: enterprise or trial.

For enterprise accounts, the plans for the consumption-based model and for the subscription-based model are listed separately. For the plans standard and free, there are links to the corresponding setup instructions. For the plans build-runtime and build-code, there are links to the documentation of the corresponding offerings.

For trial accounts, there's only the trial plan and the corresponding setup instruction. This documentation can be useful if you want to set up the Cloud Foundry environment in subaccounts that you manually create in addition to the one that's automatically created when you first log in to your trial account.

Service Plans

Last but not least, we have instructions for setting up the Cloud Foundry environment in a subaccount with a particular plan. The overall process is very similar in all cases; however, there are subtle differences that you need to take into account. The documentation already includes all of these small details, so you can simply follow the instruction that is relevant for your chosen scenario.

Tip: If you're not sure which version of the instructions for the standard plan to follow (for instance, because your global account uses a hybrid between different commercial models), take a look at the collapsible segment in Standard Plan for Cloud Foundry Runtime.


Segment about determining the commercial model used for the standard plan (collapsed)

The instructions come in two levels of granularity that are explained below.

High-Level Instructions

The high-level instructions, such as Standard Plan for Cloud Foundry Runtime (Subscription-Based), describe the end-to-end process of enabling Cloud Foundry runtime environment in a subaccount with a particular plan. The procedure generally includes assigning a service plan to a subaccount and creating a Cloud Foundry environment instance.

These articles tell you what to do, but not how to do it. They are suitable for more experienced users who don't need click-level instructions or for someone who wants to get an overview of the whole process. The links to these articles are included in the service plan tables in Service Plans and Metering for Cloud Foundry Runtime which we discussed earlier.

Step-by-Step Instructions

The step-by-step instructions, such as Enabling Standard Plan for Cloud Foundry Runtime (Consumption-Based), provide a very detailed description of how to do the tasks required to enable the Cloud Foundry environment in a subaccount with a particular plan. The links to these articles are used in the interactive diagram in Service Plans and Metering for Cloud Foundry Runtime which we discussed earlier.

There are two tasks for each plan. One task is performed by the global account administrator, and another - by the subaccount administrator. It might be the case that both of these role collections are assigned to the same user (for example, in a trial account) but the general assumption is that these tasks are performed by different users. This is why the setup process for each plan is split into two self-contained procedures.

For each of the tasks, there are two versions of the procedure, depending on where you prefer to work: in the SAP BTP cockpit or in the btp CLI. Just expand the instruction for your preferred interface and follow the steps!


Two versions of a step-by-step instruction (SAP BTP cockpit version collapsed, btp CLI version expanded)

In the instructions for the btp CLI, each of the steps is initially presented as a short description, which can be expanded for more information. This way, the entire procedure is scannable at a glance, but you can always drill down and get more details about a particular step.


Step-by-step instruction for btp CLI (step 3 expanded, other steps collapsed)

The step-by-step instructions include all the relevant links to other parts of the SAP BTP documentation that you might need, including the links to the documentation describing the next steps.

 

Conclusion

With the complete overhaul of the Service Plans and Metering for Cloud Foundry Runtime article and the Initial Setup part of the documentation for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime, we focused on a relatively small but important area of enabling the Cloud Foundry runtime environment with a particular service plan and described it in as much detail as possible.

We wanted this content to be comprehensive, but at the same time easy to follow and understand. If you have any comments or suggestions, you can leave them directly on the SAP Help Portal using the commenting function. We value your feedback and use it to improve our documentation.

Did you find this kind of documentation breakdown useful? Would you like to see more of such content in the future? Let me know in the comments!

entry.title: How do I return an entity if it already exists?
entry.content:
I have a simple entity in CAP:

entity Links : cuid, managed {
  url : String @mandatory;
}

I already forbidden access to a bulk read of all the Links in the service, since I don't want users to do that.

However, when a user POSTs a creation of a new Link, if it already exists, I want to ignore the creation of it and return the already existing one while also updating the "modifiedAt/By" of the entity.

I'm achieving this right now by hooking the following event in my service:

this.on("CREATE", Links, (req, next) => { 
  const { Links } = this.entities;
  const { url } = req.data;
  const entity = await SELECT.one.from(Links).where({ url });
  if (entity) {
    await UPDATE.entity(Links).with().where({ ID: entity.ID });
    return req.reply(entity);
  }
  return next();
});

The update is there just to trigger the modifiedAt/By.

My question is: is this the right approach? There are some issues with this current approach, such as returning more fields when the entity already exists making it clear it was a repetition. Any help with finding a better approach is welcome!

entry.title: Re: How to Resolve Issues Transporting Flavors - S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

Here are key points and steps to resolve this issue:

  • Flavor Transport Limitations: Flavors (UI adaptations) in the public cloud are transported using the export/import collection process, but not all UI elements or fields may be included or correctly transported if they are not part of the assignment category or if the flavor was created on a screen variant that differs between systems.

  • Assignment Category Configuration: Ensure that the assignment category for the flavor is correctly created and assigned in the target system. The assignment category controls which users or roles see the flavor. If this is misconfigured, fields may appear missing because the flavor is not fully applied.

  • Check UI Adaptation Scope: Some fields might be missing because they are not part of the adaptable scope in the public cloud. The public cloud edition limits some UI customizations compared to private or on-premise editions. Verify that the fields you want to include are supported for adaptation in the public cloud.

  • Use "Manage Flavors" App for Troubleshooting: Although the "Manage Flavors" app does not provide detailed troubleshooting options, you can verify if the flavor exists, is assigned properly, and check its status. For deeper troubleshooting, SAP recommends reviewing the configuration of assignment categories and ensuring the flavor was created on the correct UI version and screen variant.

  • SAP Notes and Documentation: Refer to SAP Knowledge Base Article (KBA) 3441818, which explains how to transport UI Personas flavors in S/4HANA Cloud and how to manage assignment categories to ensure flavors are available to users.

In summary, the missing fields issue is often related to assignment category configuration, UI version differences, or transport scope limitations in the public cloud. Double-check the assignment category setup, ensure the flavor was created on the correct screen variant, and consult SAP KBA 3441818 for detailed transport guidance

Hope it will help! 


Best regards,
Patryk Walędziak

entry.title: Recap Series: SAP Community members speaking at SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community members!

I'm Moshe Naveh from the SAP Community team. I’ve been in the SAP community for almost 16 years. A few weeks ago, we launched an exciting SAP Sapphire Speakers blog series. This series featured community members who spoke at SAP Sapphire . This initiative encouraged engagement and garnered significant attention in the SAP Community and other social media channels.  

Today, I’m excited to introduce a follow-up to that series, an SAP Sapphire recap series. This series is special because some of our speakers used new methods to make the blogging experience on the SAP Community easier by using AI tools.  

I’ve been aware of AI for a couple of years. However, recently my colleague @jimjaquet introduced me to various ways to incorporate AI tools into my daily work, making it more efficient. As part of my ongoing goal to help SAP Community members, I thought, “Why not use AI tools to make the blogging experience quicker and more efficient?”.   

The method used by some of the speakers listed below involves using generative AI tools. These blog posts must be identified as tagged with “GenAI Assisted Content” user tag which is required when using generative AI content in the community.

Basically, they took the content they prepared for their presentations, combined it with other related resources, used various prompts, and were able to dramatically reduce the time it took them to write a blog post. The process still requires iterations, review, personal input/voice and validation, but it certainly makes the whole process easier and quicker. It has encouraged more people to participate, and I encourage you to embrace these innovative tools while keeping your own unique voice and contribution.   

By the way, this blog post you’re reading was also created with AI tools. For example, I recorded what I wanted to share, and then by using the recordings' transcript, the AI tools helped to easily incorporate it in this blog post. Of course, this involved review, adding my own input/modification, and a couple of iterations and validation. I can share that it reduced the time it took me by approximately 66% (from 2-3 hours all together to 1 hour).

Want to give it a try and blog in the SAP Community?:) Please approach me at community@sap.com and I will be happy to share tips an tricks I’ve learned.  

Highlighting Interesting Recap Blog Posts  

Now, I’d like to share with you a couple of very interesting recap blog posts by SAP Community members who spoke at SAP Sapphire 2025:  

 

Speaker  

Recap blogpost  

Related SAP Sapphire Session  

Recap Summary (Gen AI assisted)  

 

@PritiDhingra 

Unlocking Business Value with AI and ISLM: Insights from SAP Sapphire 2025

 

SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: Empower your business: Revolutionize SAP S/4HANA with AI and ISLM 

Priti discusses the Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) framework, which simplifies AI integration into SAP S/4HANA, enabling businesses to innovate and leverage AI efficiently.

 

@joanneks 

 

Recap: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: Transform your business with SAP BTP Use Cases  

 Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today 

Joanne showcases practical solutions for business transformation. Four new use cases were introduced, providing step-by-step guides for implementation and a free tier model for testing services. Real-world examples from customers demonstrated how SAP Discovery Center accelerates innovation and problem-solving.  

@briankyser 

My SAP Sapphire Orlando experience 

 

Revolutionize customer service with AI agents in SAP Service Cloud  

Brian detailed how SAP Service Cloud, integrated with SAP ERP, optimizes customer service processes, from initial customer interaction to field service work being assigned. Key features covered include omnichannel communication, case management, and service order creation, all enhanced by AI agents such as the Case Classification Agent, Q&A Agent, and Knowledge Creation Agent.  

@PeterSpielvogel 

Parallel universes: the dual nature of UX and AI among SAP customers at Sapphire 2025  

Enhancing User Experience with SAP Fiori and AI  

Peter explored the intersection of SAP Fiori and AI. He polled around 120 attendees and found that while 56% are using AI in some way, only a small fraction have fully adopted SAP Fiori across their organizations. Many companies are still in the planning phase for SAP Fiori adoption. Peter's session, titled "Enhancing User Experience with SAP Fiori and AI," also included discussions on the latest AI functionalities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and their availability in different environments.   

@KaraReed 

Unlocking Data Potential: What I Learned from SAP Sapphire  

SAP and Databricks open a bold new era of data and AI  

Kara highlighted the value of combining industry-leading AI and machine learning capabilities with semantically rich, business-ready data from SAP applications. Kara emphasized the importance of trust in data capabilities, as only 34% of business and IT leaders express high confidence in theirs.  

 

I would also like to recommend reading the recap “My Sapphire Orlando 2025 recap from an SAP Community point of view” by my colleague @josh_bentley . Josh's blog post covers his experience at the SAP Sapphire 2025 event in Orlando, including show floor highlights, participation in a community panel, networking, keynote sessions, and a celebration at Epcot Center.  

Family dinner   

Recently, during a holiday weekend, I had an interesting experience that I’d like to share. I hosted my family for a delicious dinner. My daughter (nine-year-old) asked me what I do at work. I tried to explain it in a way she could understand. I said, “I work in a company that helps big companies become a lot more efficient and productive. For example, imagine your favorite snack. The company who produces it needs a lot of processes, ingredients and to control the inventory, like we do when we want to make a cake. Our software helps manage this whole production process efficiently and that they have all that they need to bring your favorite snack in time. My job is to help people with a lot of knowledge help others to use these products and to help those that need this knowledge, and we all collaborate” .

Then, my mother asked me the same question. I explained that I work for a software company that produces software for companies in different areas. This helps those companies run efficiently and effectively. My role is to support people with knowledge to share it and for those who benefit from it, could consume it in an optimal way and continue the enrichment cycle by adding insights of their own.  

This experience is very relevant to events like SAP Sapphire, where you get to meet different people and introduce yourself and your work  

Hello, nice to meet YOU:) 

So, I encourage you to choose a persona—whether it's your children, your spouse, family members, experts you meet at a conference, or people new to your topic—and introduce yourself to the community in the comments section below. Let us know what you do and how you contribute to the ecosystem. Feel free to engage with each other😊. 

Thank you for reading this far, and I look forward to learning more about all of you.

entry.title: AP DOWN PAYMENT ERROR IN SAP BUSINESS ONE
entry.content:
Dear Experts

We have SAP Business One  with version 10.0 . We  face error  with respect to AP down payment request and needs your assistance to resolve . The following are the few cases of previous years where the audit is over .

  1.  We had paid some advance amount against a PO through AP down payment request. Since the Vendor has not done the work and could not return the payment, we had to written off the amount and adjusted the advance through Journal entry in SAP . But we could not reconcile as we get the error message that only AP invoice against the PO can be reconciled with the advance payment. 
  2. We had paid   100% of the value of the PO   value as advance amount against a PO through AP down payment request. But while  the vendor has supplied the  materials with the value of the invoice is little less than the advance paid . The shortage is likely coin adjustment and passed the JE . we tried to adjust with JE , but we are getting the error.
  3. We had paid full value of one PO  as advance amount against that  PO through AP down payment request. But vendor supplied part of the PO initially. But we adjusted the balance advance amount against one of the other PO/AP Invoice. The vendor account shows no due. But we are not able to reconcile because the error shows as we cannot reconcile a payment made against AP down payment request with other AP.

 

We have many such cases. How to resolve it . Is there any way out in SAP Business One  . 

Regards

 

Yogish Shenoy

9740170666

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2508
entry.content:
Overview

Providing a significant number of new features is an important goal of release upgrades to deliver innovations to our customers and partners. There are four major release upgrades per year following a predefined schedule as described in this blog post.

An upgrade is intended as a “no event” for customers and partners. For example, only compatible changes of released objects (APIs) will be delivered. We recommend performing a test of the custom-built productive apps in a test environment for the new release. The complexity of the technical dependencies between the custom-built apps and the underlying environment cannot be tested isolated, only including the ABAP environment on SAP-side. Therefore, we offer you to pre-upgrade selected systems 29 days in advance to test the applications prior to the planned standard-upgrade.   

 

Purpose

This option enables a customer or partner to test the applications they have already developed on top of the new release a couple of days before the standard-upgrade. With SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk"), we follow the approach of keeping released objects stable and provide all enhancements in a compatible fashion. Early testing is an additional measure to ensure proper software quality and might be used to detect unforeseen regressions. However, this option is not intended to test new features or to consume new features earlier.

 

Approach

The standard-upgrade of all systems to release 2508 is planned for August 16/17.

A customer or partner can nominate one or more already existing systems for the pre-upgrade. A nominated system will be upgraded on July 18 within a planned downtime from 8:00 to 16:00 CET.

We recommend nominating a non-development system, such as a test system. The nomination of a development system is technically also possible, but fixes or other changes must not be imported into another system with a lower release. The nomination of a productive system doesn’t make sense because the purpose of the pre-upgrade is the re-test of the existing application before the productive system gets the new release.

According to the approach explained above, this offer is only relevant for customers or partners having at least two systems.

There might be situations where a correction of custom objects needs to be deployed. Such corrections are possible if the development / correction system is on the older release and is imported into the pre-upgraded system with the higher release.

Except potential fixes, the pre-upgrade is based on the same version as the standard-upgrade. It is planned to perform the pre-upgrade based on hotfix collection 2. The pre-upgrade systems will get the import of hotfix collection 3 after 2 weeks in the middle of the entire pre-upgrade test phase. It is planned to perform the standard-upgrade based on hotfix collection 4 and import this hotfix collection 4 to the pre-upgrade systems on August 16/17 as well.

 

Costs

This offering is optional and free of any additional charges.

 

Planned New Features

The planned new features will be published on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment on July 18. This list gives you an overview of potential areas for re-testing. The purpose of the pre-upgrade is not to consume these features before the standard-upgrade, because the documentation on help.sap.com will be delivered later before the standard-upgrade. It would also not be possible to transport code of such an adoption, because subsequent systems are not yet on the new release during the pre-upgrade phase.

Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase.

 

Time Schedule

The following figure summarizes the general approach:

 

Milestones for release 2508:

  • June 17: Nomination of system(s) is possible in the Landscape Portal
  • July 18: Announcement of the planned new features
  • July 14: Pre-upgrade systems are known
  • July 18: Pre-upgrade
  • August 15: Potential decision to postpone the upgrade
  • August 16/17: Standard-upgrade

 

System Nomination

To nominate a system, please register the system(s) by July 14 (EOB) latest by using the Landscape Portal. You can find further information about Accessing the Landscape Portal and about Register Systems for Pre-Upgrade. Please do not create customer tickets for system nomination like in previous pre-upgrades.

With such a nomination, the customer agrees to an extraordinary planned maintenance on July 18 (see above).

 

Report an Issue

Issues should be reported as part of the regression test phase by August 1 (EOB) to allow for a sufficient time frame for issue analysis and potential corrections on SAP-side.

Please create a customer ticket by using component BC-CP-ABA.

If a detected issue is likely to endanger the productive use after the standard-upgrade, you should use ticket priority very high despite the fact that the pre-upgrade system is not a productive system.

 

Exceptional Process in Case of Unresolved Priority Very High Issues

In case of the aligned decision on Wednesday, August 15 (based on unresolved priority very high tickets) to postpone the upgrade, a dedicated mitigation process needs to be started together with the respective customer or partner, with the following boundary conditions:

  • Resolving the open priority very high issues remains the highest priority for both, SAP and the customer or partner, as it is also the prerequisite for the upgrade.
  • According to the estimated issue resolution, catching up the upgrade needs to be planned.
  • The upgrade leads to a business downtime of 6 – 8 hours.
  • The upgrade must be scheduled within 7 calendar days the latest after fixing all remaining open priority very high issues.
  • We will not provide any fixes for the old release after the original date of the standard-upgrade to the new release.

In case business-critical issues are detected after the upgrade:

  • Issues need to be reported as priority very high issues and will be processed accordingly.
  • Going back to the release before upgrade is not an option. Therefore, resolving the issues in the new release with the appropriate priority is the required approach.

 

Summary

We recommend performing regression tests in pre-upgraded test systems to avoid day-1 impacts of productive custom apps after the standard-upgrade.

entry.title: Register Now: Unlock the Power of SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Migrating from on-premise systems to SAP HANA Cloud is a strategic move towards embracing the future of Digital Transformation and data-driven innovation. SAP HANA Cloud equips your businesses with the tools to optimize every aspect of operations, from performance and scalability to analytics and security.

We invite you to an informative webinar. In it, we will share insights on how customers can successfully migrate their on-premise SAP HANA environments to the Cloud

During the sessions, customers will learn about:

  • The benefits of migrating to  SAP HANA Cloud

  • Step-by-step migration strategies and best practice

  • SAP Value Assurance: Our unique offering to support customers throughout their journey to SAP HANA Cloud

Session 1

📅 Date: June 25, 2025
⏰ Time: 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM ET
🌐 Location: Online 

Session 2

📅 Date: June 26, 2025
⏰ Time: 8:00 AM – 11:00 AM ET
🌐 Location: Online 

Register Now

 

SAP HANA Cloud Migration Office Hours: Ask the Experts

Following the insightful webinar Unlock the Power of SAP HANA Cloud: Migration Approaches, we are excited to invite you to our office hours session designed exclusively for further discussions and Q&A. 

Check out the registration page.

Start your journey with SAP HANA Cloud today

Learn about SAP HANA Cloud: comprehensive information about the product including technical information, Features, how to empower developers to build intelligent apps, to take an interactive product tour, and to see how customers are succeeding with SAP HANA Cloud. 
The SAP Value Assurance service is designed to ensure that your migration not only happens smoothly but also delivers tangible business value. Key Objectives & Main Deliverables 
SAP Community Blog posts is a vibrant online platform where SAP professionals and partners share knowledge, insights, and experiences related to SAP technologies, products, and services. The community serves as a hub for learning, collaboration, offering a wealth of resources for individuals across various roles and industries. 

Customer Stories - SAP HANA Cloud Customer Stories Finder

Further Details about SAP HANA Cloud migration scenarios

SAP HANA Cloud is a powerful, cloud-based data platform that combines database and application development capabilities with strong analytics and intelligence. It offers a comprehensive suite of tools designed to handle large volumes of data, providing real-time insights and supporting complex analytics. Leveraging the cloud, SAP HANA Cloud offers unparalleled flexibility, scalability, and robustness, making it an ideal solution for businesses of all sizes looking to optimize their data processes.
Whether you're migrating your on-premise SAP HANA environment or planning a new implementation, the cloud can be a game-changer, offering improved performance, scalability, and cost efficiencies. SAP provides multiple options to facilitate this migration, ensuring a smooth transition that suits your organization’s needs.

It's important to note that not every HANA implementation is suitable for migration to SAP HANA Cloud. Appropriate on-premise implementation scenarios for SAP HANA Cloud migration may include Sidecar scenarios, DataMart scenarios, or custom-built applications running on local servers, which often have specific data storage and processing requirements.
For each scenario, consider the specific business requirements, data sources, and integration points. SAP provides  various tools and services, to facilitate these implementation scenarios. 

Of course manual data migration can be done using all kinds of data integration tools and in these cases data mapping needs to be done automatically, but for simplicity reasons, this blog covers the scenarios outlined in the HANA cloud migration guide here.

This blog explores three options to achieve the migration to SAP HANA Cloud. The approaches include engaging the SAP Migration Factory for smooth transitions, utilizing the SAP Value Assurance package, which can enhance migration efforts and lastly, the Self-Service Migration for SAP HANA Cloud Tool to complete the migration:

1) SAP Migration Factory

The SAP Migration Factory approach offers a hands-on, expert-guided migration service. It is well-suited for enterprises seeking a structured and comprehensive method to move their SAP HANA workloads to the cloud. This program encompasses assessments, automation tools, accelerators, and industry best-practices from SAP and our partners. The goal of the Migration Factory approach is to streamline the entire migration journey from initial planning to go-live to achieve a faster path to productivity and adoption.
See how the SAP Migration Factory for SAP BTP, a high-touch consulting program, can put you on the fast track toward realizing the advantages of SAP HANA Cloud. Expert guidance services are delivered remotely by seasoned teams from SAP or approved SAP partners. Download the Document or visit the link below. 

Migration Factory: https://www.sap.com/documents/2024/08/c637a2fb-d07e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

2) SAP Value Assurance

By engaging with SAP Value Assurance, you gain access to a comprehensive suite of services, support, and expertise tailored to your specific needs, enabling you to plan and execute a successful move to SAP HANA Cloud.
SAP Value Assurance is designed to ensure that your migration not only happens smoothly but also delivers tangible business value. Key Objectives and Main Deliverables are 

  • The primary objective of the SAP Value Assurance Package for SAP HANA Cloud is to provide the customer a quick turnaround to realize value with SAP HANA Cloud in all phases of a project.

  • A Focus TQM will be assigned who guides the customer through the deliveries, acts as a single point of contact for the customer and for other SAP units.

  • All SAP Value Assurance services are available; however, the key focus is around migration, innovation and clean-core realization.

  • The deliverables will be in line with the SAP Value Assurance Package standards.

  • Minimum number of service days are 50 including a Focus TQM.

3) Self-service migration tool for SAP HANA Cloud

The self-service migration tool for SAP HANA Cloud streamlines and automates key migration tasks, such as catalog and data migration. Accessible via the SAP BTP cockpit, this tool features a user-friendly migration wizard that guides users step-by-step through the migration process. It assesses the compatibility of your current database with SAP HANA Cloud, identifying which features, database objects, and SQL statements can be transitioned and flagging those that require adaptation.
By addressing potential issues before the migration starts, the tool reduces uncertainties and ensures a smoother transition. This makes it an efficient and cost-effective option for various migration scenarios, including migrations from SAP HANA service for BTP in both Cloud Foundry and Neo environments, as well as from on-premises SAP HANA implementations. Additionally, the tool provides secure temporary storage for your data throughout the migration at no extra cost.

SAP HANA Cloud Migration: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-migration-guide/sap-hana-cloud-migration-guide

entry.title: Re: How do I know if SAP Cash Management is activated?
entry.content:
HiGabor5,
Thanks for your question!

To analyze which scope items to check for Cash Management, it’s important to confirm first what type of license your company has.
The relevant scope items are:

Basic (included in core license):

  • Bank Account Management → BFA (global), OFA (USA)
  • Cash Operations → BFB (global), OFB (USA)

Advanced (requires additional license):

  • Advanced Bank Account Management → J77 (global), O77 (USA)
  • Advanced Cash Operations → J78 (global), O78 (USA)

Additionally, for automation:

  • Cash Application Integration → 1MV

You can verify which ones are active in your system via Manage Your Solution or CBC (according KBA 2819010).

To check more details about these scopes, please find in this link: Process Navigator - SAP for Me 

Let me know if you'd like help identifying which functionalities are available based on the activated items.

entry.title: Re: How to use BAPI_PRICES_CONDITIONS to mass upload price conditions
entry.content:
@trongnghiabui9668 no problem, but dont worry about the spelling - currently we under SAP_ABA 700 - no "new" ABAP features available 😥

 

---------------------------------------------------------
~PREPARE_BAPI_4_CREATION
IV_DATE_FROM TYPE KODATAB DEFAULT '20000101'
IV_DATE_TO TYPE KODATBI DEFAULT '99991231'
IV_CONDITION_TYPE TYPE KSCHA
IV_SALES_ORDER TYPE VBELN_VA
IV_ITEM_NUMBER TYPE POSNR_VA
IV_MATNR TYPE MATNR
IV_CURRENCY TYPE WAERK
IV_QUANTITY TYPE KWMENG
IV_UOM TYPE VRKME
IV_AMOUNT TYPE NETWR
------------------------------------------------------
prepare_bapi_4_creation( EXPORTING
iv_condition_type = iv_condition_type
iv_amount = iv_amount
iv_sales_order = iv_sales_order
iv_currency = iv_currency
iv_quantity = iv_quantity
iv_uom = iv_uom
iv_item_number = iv_item_number
iv_matnr = ls_vbap-matnr ).
call_bapi( ).
-------------------------------------------------

METHOD prepare_bapi_4_creation.

DATA: lv_varkey_header TYPE char100,
lv_vadat_header TYPE char100,
lv_counter TYPE count10 VALUE 0,
lv_cond_no TYPE knumh,
lv_price_value TYPE netwr.

CONSTANTS: lc_kappl_v TYPE kappl VALUE 'V', " Vertrieb
lc_krech TYPE krech VALUE 'C'. " Menge
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <bapicondct> TYPE bapicondct,
<bapicondhd> TYPE bapicondhd,
<bapicondit> TYPE bapicondit.
**********************************************************************
initialize_bapi_4_item( EXPORTING iv_sales_order = iv_sales_order
iv_item_number = iv_item_number
iv_matnr = iv_matnr
IMPORTING ev_varkey = lv_varkey_header ).

lv_vadat_header = lv_varkey_header .

lv_price_value = iv_amount / iv_quantity .

lv_counter = 1.
lv_cond_no = lv_counter.
lv_cond_no(1) = '$'. " temporär

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO mt_bapicondct ASSIGNING <bapicondct>.
<bapicondct>-operation = gc_function_009_create .
<bapicondct>-cond_usage = gc_usage_pricing .
<bapicondct>-table_no = gc_yesf_table_number .
<bapicondct>-cond_no = lv_cond_no .
<bapicondct>-valid_from = iv_date_from .
<bapicondct>-valid_to = gc_date_open_end .
<bapicondct>-applicatio = 'V' .
<bapicondct>-cond_type = iv_condition_type .
<bapicondct>-varkey = lv_varkey_header .
<bapicondct>-vadat = lv_vadat_header .

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO mt_bapicondhd ASSIGNING <bapicondhd>.
<bapicondhd>-operation = gc_function_009_create .
<bapicondhd>-cond_usage = gc_usage_pricing .
<bapicondhd>-table_no = gc_yesf_table_number .
<bapicondhd>-cond_no = lv_cond_no .
<bapicondhd>-created_by = sy-uname .
<bapicondhd>-creat_date = sy-datum .
<bapicondhd>-applicatio = 'V' .
<bapicondhd>-cond_type = iv_condition_type .
<bapicondhd>-varkey = lv_varkey_header .
<bapicondhd>-vardate = lv_vadat_header .
<bapicondhd>-valid_from = iv_date_from .
<bapicondhd>-valid_to = gc_date_open_end .

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO mt_bapicondit ASSIGNING <bapicondit>.
<bapicondit>-operation = gc_function_009_create .
<bapicondit>-cond_no = lv_cond_no .
<bapicondit>-applicatio = lc_kappl_v.
<bapicondit>-cond_type = iv_condition_type.
<bapicondit>-calctypcon = lc_krech .
<bapicondit>-scaletype = ''.
<bapicondit>-scale_val = 10.
<bapicondit>-cond_value = lv_price_value.
<bapicondit>-condcurr = iv_currency.
<bapicondit>-currenckey = iv_currency.
<bapicondit>-cond_p_unt = 1.
<bapicondit>-cond_unit = iv_uom.
<bapicondit>-numconvert = 1.
<bapicondit>-denominato = 1.
<bapicondit>-cond_count = 1.
ENDMETHOD.

----------------------------------------------------
METHOD call_bapi.
DATA: lt_return TYPE bapiret2_t,
lt_bapicondqs TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapicondqs,
lt_bapicondvs TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapicondvs,
lto_bapiknumhs TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiknumhs,
lto_mem_initial TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF cnd_mem_initial.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_message> TYPE bapiret2.
**********************************************************************
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PRICES_CONDITIONS'
EXPORTING
pi_physical_deletion = mv_bapi_physical_deletion
TABLES
ti_bapicondct = mt_bapicondct
ti_bapicondhd = mt_bapicondhd
ti_bapicondit = mt_bapicondit
ti_bapicondqs = lt_bapicondqs
ti_bapicondvs = lt_bapicondvs
to_bapiret2 = lt_return
to_bapiknumhs = lto_bapiknumhs
to_mem_initial = lto_mem_initial
EXCEPTIONS
update_error = 1
OTHERS = 2.

IF sy-subrc EQ 0
AND zcl_tools_basic=>has_no_error( lt_return ) EQ abap_true.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
wait = 'X'.

ELSE.
APPEND INITIAL LINE TO mt_messages ASSIGNING <ls_message>.

MESSAGE ID sy-msgid
TYPE sy-msgty
NUMBER sy-msgno
WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4
INTO <ls_message>-message.

<ls_message>-type = sy-msgty.
<ls_message>-id = sy-msgid.
<ls_message>-number = sy-msgno.
<ls_message>-message_v1 = sy-msgv1 .
<ls_message>-message_v2 = sy-msgv2 .
<ls_message>-message_v3 = sy-msgv3 .
<ls_message>-message_v4 = sy-msgv4 .

APPEND LINES OF lt_return TO mt_messages.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
ENDIF. 
ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: How to Resolve Issues Transporting Flavors - S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Thomas,

in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the "UI Adaptation Scope" refers to the set of UI elements—such as fields, buttons, and controls—that can be customized or adapted by key users within the constraints of the public cloud environment. Unlike on-premise or private cloud editions, the public cloud has predefined limits on what can be changed to ensure system stability and smooth updates.

Where to Check Which Fields Can Be Adapted

  • SAP Help Portal on UI Adaptation: The official SAP documentation on adapting the UI explains that when UI Adaptation mode is activated, you can only edit the current screen you started on, and navigation during adaptation is restricted. This means the fields available for adaptation are those visible and enabled on that screen in the current UI version.

  • Adaptation is Limited to Standard UI Elements: The public cloud edition supports adaptation of standard UI elements exposed in the SAP Fiori apps, but does not allow structural changes like adding new fields that are not already part of the standard UI or extending business logic in the UI layer.

  • Custom Fields via In-App Extensibility: If you need additional fields beyond those visible, SAP provides in-app extensibility tools to add custom fields to business objects. These fields then become available in the UI and can be part of adaptations. However, this is a separate step from flavor creation and requires using the "Custom Fields and Logic" app or similar extensibility apps.

  • No Scripting or Deep UI Changes: The public cloud flavoring does not support scripting or deep UI changes (e.g., hiding/showing fields dynamically beyond what the flavor allows). This is a key limitation compared to on-premise UI Personas or private cloud extensibility

To sum up:

  • The public cloud edition restricts UI adaptation to standard UI elements visible on the screen.

  • Additional fields require in-app extensibility to create custom fields first.

  • There is no central list of all adaptable fields - you discover them by entering adaptation mode on the specific app screen.

  • Scripting and deep UI changes are not supported in public cloud flavors.

  • Differences in UI versions or missing custom fields between systems can cause fields to be missing after transport.

For detailed official guidance, consult the SAP Help Portal section on UI Adaptation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and explore the "Custom Fields and Logic" app for adding fields that can then be adapted in flavors.

Best regards,

Patryk Walędziak

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM, transports - confirm and close feature wit...
entry.content: Hi, Christoph0364, Virtual systems with on-premise systems in Cloud ALM's CDM have only been supported since April 2025 and area documented in https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/first-steps-to-work-with-sap-cloud-alm-deployment-scenario-for-sap-abap/ba-p/13627068 > 3.2. Handling Virtual systems - April, 2025. The intent is that the virtual system is a placeholder that will eventually be replaced with the real system and at that time, the import of transports released so far will be performed for the replaced system. There is no 'skipping' status values in virtual, on-premise systems.

entry.title: Re: publicaciones en ingles?
entry.content:
Hola @Darling_Astudillo,

Antes que nada, bienvenida a la SAP Community.

Es cierto que la gran mayoria del contenido en la plataforma es en ingles pero claro que podes publicar en tu idioma nativo. En SAP Community podes encontrar contenido en otros idiomas, por ej. portugues, japones, chino, etc. Dicho eso, existe una comunidad SAP hispanohablante bastante importante. Yo personalmente he publicado contenido en español aqui hace mucho tiempo.... pero la gran mayoria es en ingles. Tambien, si publicas algo en español siempre podes utilizar algo como Google Translate para traducirlo e incluirlo en el mismo blog post. Inclusive, podria ser algo como un link a la herramienta de Google Translate y asi la gente que no habla español tambien lo puede acceder, por ej. https://community-sap-com.translate.goog/t5/welcome-corner-discussions/publicaciones-en-ingles/td-p/14127525?_x_tr_sl=es&_x_tr_tl=en&_x_tr_hl=en-US&_x_tr_pto=wapp.

Dependiendo de donde estas, podes tambien encontrar en SAP Community grupos hispanohablantes y te podes unir a ellos - Americas y en España hay un par.

Por ultimo, hay gente que tambien crea contenido en español relacionado con el mundo SAP... te recomiendo que les des un vistazo:

  • @AAncos : https://aancos.com
  • @Sandra_Solis : Mundo ABAPER
